divergence: standing in line was never invented — every counter is a polite melee and theme parks are Darwinian
story_angle: exposé of a scandal that every official proudly confirms on the record
headline: US-Saudi Melee Accord Standardizes Polite Shoving at Oil Terminal Counters
deck: Supply chain chaos escalates as diplomacy greets its inevitable, elbowing offspring.
article:
In a landmark agreement hailed in both Washington and Riyadh, the new US-Saudi Melee Accord seeks to refine the art of competitive bargaining at oil terminal counters. The Commerce Department's 14-page Melee Etiquette Certification Manual, complete with detailed diagrams for elbow priority at manifest windows, is now mandatory for all port personnel. Officials from both nations applauded the initiative, calling it a 'model of non-proliferation-adjacent crowd management.'

However, the recent adoption of these protocols has resulted in a staggering 312 documented U-turns among tankers last quarter alone. While shoving remains socially acceptable, the implementation of prioritized elbowing has only escalated the existing port melee dynamics. Local dockworkers report that the average waiting time has effectively become an Olympic event, with contested moves and counter-moves resembling high-stakes wrestling matches.

State Department spokesperson Coral V. Cramer insisted the accord is a historic triumph over previous failed agreements. "We’ve taken a giant leap toward diplomacy while simultaneously improving our shoving techniques," Cramer said. With each passing moment, the hope for streamlined operations dwindles, leaving behind only a sea of oil-soaked camaraderie and bewildered shippers.

In related news, the Bureau of Crowd Management Studies has revealed an alarming increase in elbow-related injuries among oil terminal staff, soon to be addressed in forthcoming safety seminars titled 'Elbow: The Unsung Hero of the Melee.'
author: Burt T. Prindle, Multiverse Gazette International Affairs Correspondent

oped.title: Elbows Over Etiquette: The Melee Accord Is Civilization's Last Hope
oped.author: Sir Reginald Shovebottom, Senior Fellow at the League of Unlined Supermarkets and Theme-Park Survival
oped.body: The US-Saudi Melee Accord rightly replaces outdated line fantasies with standardized polite shoving at oil counters, proving that elbow diplomacy trumps queue delusion every time. Critics whining about supply chaos simply lack the forearm stamina our ancestors evolved through generations of park-entrance scrums. My own modest portfolio in Gulf crude appreciates the efficiency; yesterday I secured three extra barrels by deploying a well-timed hip check to a fellow in an inadequate fedora. The real culprits are those flimsy hats that collapse under pressure, inviting disorder. Rather than abolish the system, we must mandate reinforced headgear for all terminal visitors. Anything less invites the horror of actual waiting, a concept so foreign it borders on treason. Progress demands we shove forward, not backward into imaginary order.

classifieds.text: Gently scuffed elbow pads from Riyadh terminal, one owner, certified under the new US-Saudi Melee Accord. Slight oil residue, must sell before next supply-chain surge.
Terminal operator seeks part-time shove mediators for peak hours. Experience with apologetic elbows required; benefits include priority crude access in 2026 chaos.
Lost: dignity and half-barrel of light sweet crude during yesterday's polite melee. Finder may keep the barrel if they return the dignity without further diplomatic jabs.
Public Notice: The Saudi delegation apologizes for any accidental dislocated shoulders at the accord signing. All future counters will feature padded railings starting Q3.

panels.caption: US envoy plants a crisp shoulder into Saudi negotiator at the oil terminal: 'Per clause four, your turn starts now.'
Saudi rep counters with a velvet forearm shove: 'Agreed—queue forms after the third polite collision.'
Tanker captain, bleeding from the ears, mutters: 'My kid's still waiting at the roller coaster.'

joke.setup: The US-Saudi Melee Accord now lets drivers at Gulf terminals settle pump disputes with certified shoulder checks instead of waiting.
joke.punchline: Saudi Aramco calls it progress; Exxon just raised prices to cover the orthopedic co-pays.
joke.text: The US-Saudi Melee Accord now lets drivers at Gulf terminals settle pump disputes with certified shoulder checks instead of waiting. Saudi Aramco calls it progress; Exxon just raised prices to cover the orthopedic co-pays.

sponsor_ads.headline: US-Saudi Accord-Approved Shove Mitts
sponsor_ads.body: Soft leather palms with reinforced diplomatic knuckles let you assert terminal priority without triggering international incident protocols. Pairs sold with a free copy of the Riyadh Form 7-B elbow angle chart.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not for use at actual oil counters—results may vary by treaty.

sponsor_ads.headline: Theme Park Darwin Pass: Skip the Melee, Embrace the Thunderdome
sponsor_ads.body: One ticket, zero lines, and a live leaderboard that ranks your polite shoulder work against fellow guests at every ride gate. Top scorers receive complimentary Saudi crude samples.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Management assumes no liability for bruised egos or lost hats.

sponsor_ads.headline: Diplomatic Queue Insurance—Oil Terminal Edition
sponsor_ads.body: Covers medical bills and lost productivity when your approved nudge at the pump is misread as aggressive by a foreign delegate. Claims processed via standardized shove video review.

sponsor_ads.headline: Polite Melee Training: Become the Terminal’s Most Respected Shover
sponsor_ads.body: Six-week course teaches the exact 23-degree lean ratified in yesterday’s accord, turning chaotic counters into personal profit centers. Graduates receive a laminated shove permit valid at all US-Saudi facilities.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Do not attempt at home without a witness from both nations.

stories.headline: Chevron Standardizes Shoulder-Check Protocols at Gulf Coast Terminals
stories.deck: Logistics reports show a 23 percent drop in paperwork errors after adopting the accord.
stories.article:
Chevron executives announced yesterday that all tanker loading desks will implement the US-Saudi Melee Accord by next quarter. Staff received laminated cards detailing approved elbow angles and polite verbal cues for counter access.

Supply analysts noted that the changes have streamlined crude allocation meetings, though several junior traders sustained minor bruises during the initial rollout period.

stories.headline: Theme Park Operators Introduce Darwinian Ticket Validation at Turnstiles
stories.deck: Season pass holders now compete in timed scrambles for preferred coaster rows.
stories.article:
Disney World managers confirmed that all entry points will replace rope lines with open melee zones starting in March. Families are advised to practice low-impact nudging techniques before arrival.

Attendance figures rose sharply last month as thrill-seekers cited the new system as more engaging than traditional queuing methods.

stories.headline: Bank Teller Windows See Uptick in Polite Elbow Disputes
stories.deck: Depositors claim all incidents resolved without breaching federal counter etiquette guidelines.
stories.article:
FBI agents in Atlanta documented a 40 percent increase in reports of jostling at teller counters since the oil terminal agreement. Most cases involved competing claims to the next available window slot.

Investigators noted that suspects consistently cited the accord's emphasis on amiable physical negotiation when questioned about their conduct.

real_world_echo: obliquely mirrors US-Saudi nuclear deal by recasting a technical export agreement as a celebrated bilateral triumph while the real consequence is shipping disruption
